title: Air-fryer pork tenderloin
subtitle: A whole pork fillet seasoned and cooked in 20 minutes
excerpt: Pork tenderloin rubbed with smoked paprika, garlic powder, and thyme, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 18 to 20 minutes. The tenderloin is the leanest cut on the pig and the air fryer's intense heat seals the outside quickly, keeping the interior moist at 63°C.
sourceNotes: Pork tenderloin (called pork fillet in British butchery) is the muscle running along the inside of the spine. It is the least-worked muscle on the pig and therefore the most tender. It has almost no fat, which means it overcooks quickly. The air fryer is well-suited to it because the high circulating heat cooks it fast, minimising the window during which the lean meat can dry out.

content.text: Remove the silver skin before seasoning. The silver skin is a thin strip of white sinew running along one side of the tenderloin. It does not break down with heat and will cause the tenderloin to curl and cook unevenly. Slide a sharp knife under the silver skin, angle the blade slightly upward, and pull it away from the meat.

items.name: Pork tenderloin
items.prepNote: about 300 to 350g each, silver skin removed

content.text: Remove the silver skin from both {{pork-tenderloin}} fillets. Brush all over with {{olive-oil}}. Mix the {{smoked-paprika}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{thyme}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} and rub all over both fillets. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Place both fillets in the basket. Cook at 200°C for 18 to 20 minutes, turning once at the 10-minute mark. The internal temperature should reach 63 to 65°C for a moist result. Rest for 5 minutes before slicing on the diagonal into medallions.

items.fix: Pork tenderloin has almost no fat to protect it from overcooking. Remove from the air fryer at 63°C and rest immediately. The temperature will rise 2 to 3 degrees during resting
items.cause: Tenderloin cooked past 65°C, or silver skin not removed causing uneven cooking
items.symptom: Pork dry and chalky despite reaching 63°C

items.fix: Even a small strip of silver skin causes the tenderloin to bow. Check the full length of both fillets and remove any remaining connective tissue before cooking
Reduce temperature to 180°C and cook for an additional 5 minutes. Thicker centre-cut tenderloins take longer; the air fryer surface-browns faster than the heat penetrates a thick piece
items.cause: Fillets thicker than average, or placed too close to the heating element
items.symptom: Exterior dark but centre below 63°C after 20 minutes

content.text: Air-fryer pork tenderloin slices into medallions that work well alongside most sides. Serve with roasted root vegetables and a mustard cream sauce, with apple sauce and roast potatoes, or sliced into a salad. A full tenderloin feeds four from a 20-minute cook, making it one of the most efficient proteins in the air fryer.
